Once complete this work will adhere to the customer request, ensure our network can uphold the increased electricity usage in your area and it will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the network. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place in the Wheatsheaf Road area in Henfield.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified cables that need upgrading and 4 leaning poles that need replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our engineers may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. We will need access to the inside of your property to conduct safety checks in the morning before we switch off the power and we will need to do another check when we turn the power back on. This work is taking place on High Street. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so Talie, Stuart and Lisa are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

We know it's frustrating being without power, but we wanted to assure you we're still here and as soon as we have some more information we'll update you again.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 15:58 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the Bushey Road area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 21:00 and 22:00, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover 4 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place near Hall Farm in Pudding and adjacent to Ryburgh Road in Fakenham.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts recently. This work will cover approximately 400 metres across 5 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place in the Thorpe Market Road area in Roughton.

Sent by David at 12:31 - Hi David here again. We can now confirm that all bar 1 property in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to safely repair a damaged overhead cable that was laying on the ground caused by another company in the High Street area.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 12:13 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 11:16 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the High Street area in the interest of the public's health and safety. This is so they can investigate reports of an overhead cable that is on the ground due to another company.

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

However, based on an average for this kind of issue, our estimated time to have you back on is between 12:30 and 13:30. 

We'll keep you updated with our progress, but in the meantime our team are always here to talk on 105 if you have any questions or concerns.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: These works were originally due to take place on the 26th June 2026 but had to be cancelled due to a local event taking place on the same day. We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine a transformer on our electricity poles to check it is functioning properly. This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place on New House Lane.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We already have staff on site in the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather.
To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our contractors Ground Control need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 600 metres across 10 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, A longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out.. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages as we appreciate you may have been impacted by an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Wooley Road.

Hello, Aaron from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today, our field staff and control room engineers are working to get your power back on as quickly as they can.

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently. If you have, we're sorry for further disruption. All power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

We know that not everyone affected by today's power cut will have had other power cuts recently, but we want you to know that if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations.

Sent by Aaron at 14:40 - Thanks for your patience this afternoon.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our underground cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Canterbury CT2 0/CT3 4 area to investigate the power cut affecting 32 properties. 

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. 

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR

Sent by Aaron at 14:20 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground network fault that initially interrupted power to 2127 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 32 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 17:00-18:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified cables and 4 poles that need replacing and upgrading. We are also planning on installing 2 new poles. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. We will need access to the inside of your property to conduct safety checks in the morning before we switch off the power and we will need to do another check when we turn the power back on. This work is taking place on Letzen Road. Give and take boards with a temporary 15 minute obstruction will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 12:25 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover 3 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place on Mapletree Lane in Mill Green.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work will cover approximately 300 metres across 4 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place in Ryes Lane in Hatfield Heath.

Sent by Aaron at 15:21 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

MainMessage: We are currently experiencing problems with the electricity supply in your area.  Our engineers have attended the fault, and have made an emergency repair to restore power to as many premises as possible.  It is our intention to return to make a permanent repair as soon as we can.  I'm sorry, but we can't give you a time for this at present. Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 03:54 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers repair a fault on the underground network in the Lea Bridge Road area, we've now restored power to most of the affected properties. There are now only 3 properties without power, who we'll speak to directly. If you're without power and haven't been contacted, then we'll need to hear from you. Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

We know that being without power for a long time can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the unexpected outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 02:31 - Our engineers have just called in to advise us that underground repairs are taking longer than expected.

This means it's going to take us past our current time so we are now working towards between 06:00-07:00 to have your power back on.

We know how disruptive being without power can be, we are working as fast as we can. If you're worried about anyone who may be more vulnerable in your property who is affected by todays outage, please don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Decasta at 01:27 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather.
To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. This work is taking place along Common Road.
